Anneliese R. Vogel-Strauss
Curriculum Vitae
Department of History of Art, Carrowmore University
14 Quadrangle Walk, Edinburgh EH8 9YL, United Kingdom
a.vogelstrauss@carrowmore.ac.uk | +44 131 496 0224

Academic Appointments
Reader in Early Modern Visual Culture, Carrowmore University, 2021–present
Lecturer in History of Art, Carrowmore University, 2016–2021
Leverhulme Early Career Fellow, Courtauld Institute of Art, 2013–2016

Education
Ph.D., History of Art, University of Oxford, 2013
Dissertation: Pigment and Piety: Venetian Colour Workshops and the Devotional Image, 1480–1560
Supervisors: Prof. Gervase Rosser and Prof. Geraldine A. Johnson
M.St., History of Art and Visual Culture, University of Oxford, 2008
B.A. (Hons), Art History and Italian, University of Glasgow, 2007

Books
1. Vogel-Strauss, Anneliese R. 2022. Pigment and Piety: Colour, Commerce, and Devotion in Renaissance Venice. New Haven: Yale University Press. Winner of the 2023 Renaissance Society of America Phyllis Goodhart Gordan Book Prize.
2. Vogel-Strauss, Anneliese R., and Tomas Eriksen, eds. 2019. Workshop Economies: Materials and Makers in Early Modern Europe. Amsterdam: Amsterdam University Press.

Refereed Journal Articles
3. Vogel-Strauss, Anneliese R. 2024. "Ultramarine Contracts: Pricing the Sacred in Cinquecento Venice." The Art Bulletin 106(2): 88–112. doi:10.1080/00043079.2024.2301157.
4. Vogel-Strauss, Anneliese R. 2020. "The Apprentice's Hand: Attribution and Labour in Tintoretto's Workshop." Renaissance Quarterly 73(4): 1188–1226. doi:10.1017/rqx.2020.221.

Anneliese R. Vogel-Strauss page 2 of 3

5. Vogel-Strauss, Anneliese R. 2017. "Smalt and Substitution: Cheap Blues in Northern Altarpieces." Burlington Magazine 159(1372): 540–548. www.burlington.org.uk/archive/article/smalt-substitution.

Honors and Awards
Phyllis Goodhart Gordan Book Prize, Renaissance Society of America, 2023
Carrowmore University Teaching Excellence Award, 2019
AHRC Doctoral Studentship, 2009–2012

Languages
English (native), German (native), Italian (fluent), Latin (reading), Dutch (reading)

References
Prof. Gervase Rosser, University of Oxford, gervase.rosser@hoa.ox.ac.uk
Prof. Evelyn Welch, University of Bristol, e.welch@bristol.ac.uk
Dr. Tomas Eriksen, Rijksmuseum Amsterdam, t.eriksen@rijksmuseum.nl
